title: The continuity index is a dated snapshot; standalone continuity records are the additive current corpus
summary: The file named continuity-index.json was generated on 2026-07-12 and contains records CM-0001 through CM-0009. Later continuity work is retained as standalone files under operations/continuity/records, including CM-1004 committed on 2026-07-13. Therefore absence from the dated index is not evidence that a later memory record does not exist. Consumers must treat the index as a historical snapshot unless a materialized corpus manifest explicitly includes the standalone record set.

status_relations.observed: The dated index ends at CM-0009 while a later committed standalone record CM-1004 exists.
status_relations.inferred: Repository consumers that inspect only continuity-index.json can produce false absence conclusions about later project memory.
status_relations.proposed: Add a deterministic non-authoritative continuity corpus manifest that enumerates the snapshot index plus standalone record files with source blob SHAs and duplicate-ID detection.
status_relations.superseded: Any operational assumption that continuity-index.json is an exhaustive live registry is superseded; its historical contents remain valid for its generated_at time.
status_relations.unresolved: No committed corpus manifest was identified during this run, and the complete standalone-record directory was not enumerated through a tree-listing API. Exhaustive corpus membership therefore remains unproven.

falsification_or_review_route: Fetch continuity-index.json at blob efb8b5ca41a00a3484af1df112a81fc6a1b6d63a and verify generated_at plus its final record ID. Fetch CM-1004.json at blob b9ca7f18ede671976b9eeae657f2547545a5442e and its creation commit. Falsify this record if the index already references CM-1004 or if a newer authoritative manifest explicitly declares and proves exhaustive corpus membership.
